page: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance allocates 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU allocation is shared. It includes a 1600 GB local NVMe SSD for storage and provides one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data shows top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, while multi-core performance falls in the average tier. Memory performance is a notable strength, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 25%, supported by a read-only small block cached memory bandwidth of 2241.95 GB/sec. Large block uncached read and write bandwidths are 131.64 GB/sec and 67.64 GB/sec, respectively. For language model workloads, text generation using a small 135M model achieves a strong rating at 263.35 tokens per second.

The server offers high memory density with 8.0 GB of RAM per core, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources. However, the shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff for sustained multi-core compute tasks. Given the 128.0 GB memory capacity and 1600 GB NVMe SSD, this configuration is well-suited for database operations, caching layers, memory-intensive web serving, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: The Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 16 shared vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a 1600 GB local NVMe SSD. Powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz, this x86_64 architecture instance features 8 physical cores with hyper-threading. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory performance, with PassMark memory scores in the top 25%. Large block uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 131.64 GB/sec. While the shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained multi-core processing, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core and substantial local NVMe storage make this server qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for database hosting, caching systems, and memory-intensive web serving applications.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an AMD EPYC server with 16 shared vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 1600 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong memory performance and top-tier single-core CPU benchmarks.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd features 16 shared vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 1600 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors, 128 GB RAM, and high-capacity local NVMe storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 2.0 GHz processor
128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage bundled
Top-tier single-core CPU and strong PassMark memory benchmark performance
Shared CPU allocation optimized for memory-heavy, bursty workloads
Ideal for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive web serving
